El Museo del Barrio is a small cultural museum in East Harlem, located along Museum Mile just across from Central Park. It celebrates Latin American and Caribbean art, with a special focus on Puerto Rican heritage.
The museum is spread across one floor with two galleries. One of the galleries, down the hall, was nearly empty during my visit with only a few artworks on display and big TV. 
 I really enjoyed the main gallery, which featured the exhibitions Mestre Didi: Spiritual Form; highlighting the work of a renowned Brazilian artist and spiritual leader, and Candida Alvarez: Circle, Point, Hoop, showcasing pieces by the Puerto Rican-American artist.
